design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate 2D sidescrolling levels by jumping and gliding, collecting marbles, using power-ups like the Nerf Ballzooka gun, and defeating enemies across themed historical-era virtual worlds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Non-linear stage selection coupled with accessible difficulty designed for broad appeal, creating tension between player choice and guided progression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as a wisecracking anthropomorphic bobcat (Bubsy) who rescues family members from a corrupt entrepreneur's theme park while exploring themed worlds and wielding comedic weapons
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately designed as more approachable and humorous than the first game, prioritizing character personality and non-linear exploration over challenge; original creator Michael Berlyn criticized the design direction as misunderstanding the franchise's core vision

difficulty.lives_system: Bubsy can absorb three hits before losing a life, significantly more forgiving than the first game's single-hit mechanic
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players choose stage order from available levels, potentially allowing them to tackle stages in preference order; reviewers noted the game is 'boringly easy for experienced players,' suggesting minimal difficulty scaling

progression.upgrade_system: Marbles serve as collectible currency; Nerf Ballzooka gun and other special items function as temporary power-ups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Non-linear stage selection system allows players to choose which levels to play, unlike the first game's strict linearity
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer mode with second player controlling Bubsy's younger relatives

world_building_techniques: Themed historical-era virtual worlds within the Amazatorium setting
Character-driven humor via Bubsy's personality and voice acting
TV pilot characters integrated into game narrative
Antagonist motivation (Oinker P. Hamm stealing historical items via time machine)

faction_indicators: Bubsy family unit (protagonist, niece Terri, nephew Terry, sidekick Arnold)
Corrupt entrepreneur Oinker P. Hamm as centralized antagonist
danger_signals: Theme park setting used as front for historical theft via time machine
Corrupted public figures (Oinker P. Hamm's deception of the public)
environmental_storytelling: Amazatorium theme park designed around stolen historical artifacts
Virtual world zones representing different historical eras
Virtual reality technology as plot device for theft and deception
